preview_text:
The model favors Iran at 49%, while Egypt has a 22% chance of victory. This 27-percentage-point gap reflects Iran as the heavy favorite in the model's assessment. Egypt's underdog position carries meaningful but minority odds.

The draw emerges as the second-most-likely outcome at 30%, suggesting the match could be closely contested despite Iran's modeled edge. The win probabilities are spread across all three outcomes rather than concentrated, indicating competitive balance in terms of possible match shape, even if one team is favored on average.

Model and market diverge substantially. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both assign Iran only 27%, roughly 22 percentage points below the model. Conversely, they rate Egypt at 42%, almost double the model's 22%. Both consensus sources agree closely with each other and converge on a draw near 31%. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these competing views, but the divergence is large enough to warrant attention from those comparing forecasts.
